findings.description: int((ref - moment).total_seconds()) truncates toward zero, so a moment less than 1 second in the future yields seconds==0 and skips the 'if seconds < 0' branch, returning 'just now' instead of 'in the future' — contradicts the explicit future-handling intent and is reachable under normal clock skew.

clamp_to_range omits the tz-awareness guard that humanize_delta enforces; passing a naive value with aware bounds (or vice versa) raises a bare TypeError from min/max instead of the module's own ValueError, an inconsistent and unhelpful contract.

start_of_day (and day_range/business_days_between) are typed `date` but datetime is a subclass and passes through silently; reading .year/.month/.day drops the time component and tzinfo, causing silent data loss rather than an error.

humanize_delta's weeks bucket is the unbounded terminal branch, so multi-year deltas render as large 'N w ago' (e.g. '520 w ago'); acceptable under the 'coarse' docstring but a usability limitation worth noting.

Docstring claims 'strictly' parses YYYY-MM-DD, but strptime accepts non-zero-padded inputs like '2024-1-5'; minor docstring/behavior mismatch ('strictly' most plausibly means 'raises rather than returning a sentinel', which holds).
The name `seconds` is reassigned as the divmod remainder, shadowing the original variable; harmless readability nit.
